title: Computation as a Universal and Fundamental Concept
description: TIM ROUGHGARDEN treats computation as a law of nature, tracing its arc from Turing's proof that some problems are unsolvable to the P versus NP question at the heart of modern computer science.

title: A digestion of unit distance constructions
description: Suppose that one has a set of points in the plane, which we will think of as the complex plane . Let denote the number of unit distances determined by these points, i.e., pairs of points whose displacement obeys the equation (For this discussion it will not b…

title: Appointment of Regius Professor of Mathematics in the University of Oxford: 1 July 2026
description: The King has approved that Professor James Maynard FRS, Professor of Number Theory at the Mathematical Institute, University of Oxford, be appointed Regius Professor of Mathematics in the University of Oxford in succession to Professor Sir Andrew Wiles.

title: The Riemann Hypothesis manifested in dynamical quantum phase transitions
description: The authors establish a formal correspondence between the Riemann Hypothesis and engineered quantum many-body dynamics. By constructing a Riemann-based Hamiltonian, they demonstrate that the hypothesis manifests through quantum phase transitions.

title: Spreading the Gospel of Theoretical Computer Science to an Omega(1) Fraction of Humanity: My Trevisan Award Acceptance Speech at STOC

title: After 80 Years, Mathematicians Give Famed ‘Erdős Method’ an Upgrade

title: The Riemann Hypothesis – interactive explanation
description: An interactive journey from 'what is a prime number?' to a genuine understanding of the most important unsolved problem in mathematics.
